title: Executive Order 7915—Amendment of Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A comprehensive restatement of the entire Civil Service Rules (Rules I-XVI), implementing the 1883 Civil Service Act with extensive, detailed procedural protections throughout (examination standards, veteran preference, removal due process) rather than a novel grant of executive discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extremely detailed, clear institutional roles (independent Civil Service Commission, department heads, appointing officers).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Various discretionary standards (e.g., Rule XII's 'such cause as will promote the efficiency of the service' for removals) are broad but reflect well-established, longstanding personnel-law standards, not novel vague grants, and are surrounded by extensive written-notice and response procedures.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The master, standing civil-service rulebook — a permanent regulatory framework by design, not an emergency grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Rule XII, Section 4 expressly limits the Civil Service Commission's own jurisdiction to review removals: 'the Commission shall have no jurisdiction to review the findings of a removing officer... nor shall the Commission have authority to investigate any removal or reduction, unless it is alleged... that the procedure... has not been followed, or that the removal was made for political or religious reasons.' This narrows the standing independent oversight body's own review authority to procedural and discrimination claims only, foreclosing substantive merits review of individual removal decisions.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: Preserves the existing, previously-itemized Schedule A/B exemption categories (already individually scrutinized via dozens of amendment orders throughout this stream) rather than creating new unbounded exemptions.

title: Executive Order 7916—Extending the Competitive Classified Civil Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Historically significant order (the 1938 civil-service 'blanketing-in' order): while it broadly extends merit protection by covering most previously-excepted positions into competitive service, Section 1 simultaneously reserves to the President an ongoing, self-defined power to declare future exemptions for 'policy-determining positions' and 'other positions which special circumstances require should be exempted' via subsequent Executive Orders — an open-ended, not-yet-enumerated category of executive discretion to redraw the civil-service line at will, replacing the previously fixed, itemized Schedule A/B system.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Section 2 requires agency-head certification and Civil Service Commission review/recommendation before any position is designated exempt — a genuine, if procedurally modest, accountability check on the exemption-designation power.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The 'special circumstances require' and 'necessity and justification' standards governing future exemption designations (Sec. 1-2) are genuinely undefined at the time of this order, deferring the actual boundary of the merit system to later, unspecified Executive Orders rather than fixing it now.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: A standing, permanent civil-service-coverage framework, evergreen by design.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order adds oversight layers (a new Council of Personnel Administration, agency Directors of Personnel, CSC review of exemptions) rather than removing existing ones.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Section 3's mechanism — incumbents of positions newly covered into competitive service may retain their jobs via agency-head certification of satisfactory service plus a noncompetitive exam, without open competition — converts what were previously non-merit (in many cases effectively political) appointments into protected civil-service status for sitting incumbents. This is the historically well-documented 'burrowing in' effect for which this order is widely discussed: it functionally insulates a cohort of incumbent employees from removal by a future administration, a genuine second-order structural effect distinct from the order's stated merit-extension purpose.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: Section 9 explicitly supersedes the previously fixed, itemized Schedule A/B exemption lists with new schedules to be 'set forth in subsequent Executive orders' — at the time of this order, the actual boundary of what remains excepted from competitive service is not yet defined, a less transparent architecture than the specific, itemized exemption lists this stream has otherwise treated as a positive design feature (cf. EO 7845's Part III).

title: Executive Order 7963—Authorizing Initial Appointments to Certain Executive Positions in the Wage and Hour Division in the Department of Labor Without Compliance With the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Bounded to five specifically enumerated top executive positions in a brand-new statutory division (implementing the Fair Labor Standards Act), limited to initial appointments only rather than an ongoing hiring authority, and subject to the Civil Service Commission's own qualification-establishment requirement in each case.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, bounded positions, retained CSC qualification-review check, explicit no-status proviso.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Avoids competitive process for these initial hires, though narrowly bounded to a one-time agency-startup context.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Expressly limited to 'initial appointments' only — once made, the exception is exhausted, unlike an ongoing bypass authority.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The retained CSC qualification-review requirement and the Administrator's on-record recommendation together supply a meaningful accountability anchor.

title: Executive Order 7964—Instruction of Citizens of the American Republics at Educational Institutions and Schools Maintained and Administered by the Government of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements a specific 1938 statute, requiring each participating department to draft regulations with explicit mandatory content (institution/course lists, enrollment caps, qualification standards, national-defense safeguards), with an explicit non-displacement proviso protecting U.S. citizen admissions and numeric caps (one student per republic at the military/naval academies).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, multi-layered review (department drafts regulations, State Department reviews and transmits, foreign governments apply through diplomatic channels).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Detailed, bounded requirements for each department's regulations; not left to open interpretation.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing implementation of a permanent statute.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; explicit safeguards for national-defense information and U.S. citizen admission priority.

title: Executive Order 7965—Regulations To Give Effect to Articles I and II of the Convention Providing for the Recovery and Return of Stolen or Embezzled Motor Vehicles, Trailers, Airplanes or Component Parts of Any of Them, Concluded on October 6, 1936, Between the United States of America and the United Mexican States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements a bilateral treaty via precisely enumerated, symmetric documentary requirements for both governments; narrow and reciprocal.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, detailed, itemized document requirements.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to the life of the underlying treaty, which itself has a defined termination mechanism (30 days' notice after the first year).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Clean, reciprocal, treaty-bounded coordination between the U.S. and Mexico.

title: Executive Order 7972—Prescribing Regulations Governing Payment of Losses Sustained by Officers, Enlisted Men, and Employees of the United States While in Service in Foreign Countries on Account of Appreciation of Foreign Currencies

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extensive but tightly bounded technical compensation scheme implementing a specific statute; rate-setting authority is delegated to the Secretary of State subject to annual review and Presidential approval, not concentrated in unreviewable executive discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Built-in annual review-and-approval cycle (Secretary of State reviews basic rates and submits proposed changes to the President) is itself a designed accountability mechanism, not a gap.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single unified subject (currency-appreciation loss reimbursement); the order's many sections (definitions, computation method, basic-rate table, payment method, effective date) all serve one integrated purpose rather than combining unrelated matters.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Extremely detailed, itemized definitions ('net salary,' 'net allowances,' 'country of assignment,' 'substantial period of time,' etc.) and a fully specified country-by-country basic-rate table leave minimal room for inconsistent application.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing compensation regulation subject to mandatory annual rate review, ordinary maintenance rather than an unreviewed permanent grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; the order explicitly and transparently supersedes and consolidates five prior amending orders (EO 6928, 7312, 7403, 7547, 7766, 7785, 7955) by full citation, a diff-aware consolidation rather than a silent override.

title: Executive Order 7980—Postponement of Effective Date of Certain Provisions of Executive Order No. 6166 of June 10, 1933

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight

title: Executive Order 7981—Amendment of Subdivision IV, Schedule B, Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, bounded amendment applicable only to unclassified-laborer positions within the Ordnance Department at Large, War Department.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Order is expressly recommended by the Civil Service Commission itself, on the basis of a disclosed agreement with the War Department to stop future unclassified-laborer hiring in that unit — a clear, on-record accountability anchor.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Creates a standing noncompetitive-promotion channel (advancement 'upon noncompetitive examination' from the unclassified laborer register to classified positions), which — while offset by the War Department's disclosed commitment to stop future unclassified hiring at the entry point — still allows incumbents to bypass full competitive examination for classified status on an ongoing basis.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The amendment to Schedule B is a permanent standing rule change with no stated expiration or sunset; it recurs each time a qualifying incumbent seeks promotion.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Subject to Civil Service Commission approval for each individual promotion, and the promotion explicitly does not confer transferable classified status to other branches of the federal service — a meaningful, on-record accountability anchor consistent with this stream's recommendation-anchored bypass template (cf. EO 7619, 7633, 7710, 7778, 7827, 7899, 7919), now an eighth confirmed instance.

title: Executive Order 7984—Extension of Trust Periods on Indian Lands Expiring During Calendar Year 1939

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Broad in scope (applies to 'any Indian lands, whether of a tribal or individual status' nationwide) but exercised under long-standing, repeatedly-used statutory extension authority (acts of 1887, 1906, 1917) rather than a novel assertion of power; each extension is a fixed 25-year term, not indefinite.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The order itself carves out and preserves Congress's reserved authority ('not intended to apply to any case in which the Congress has specifically reserved to itself authority to extend the period of trust') — a self-limiting, accountability-preserving proviso.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Extends trust status by a fixed 25-year term rather than indefinitely; a defined future date at which the question must be revisited, consistent with a protective (not evasive) design.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; the Congressional reservation-of-authority carve-out affirmatively preserves the existing check.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A single blanket order substitutes one uniform 25-year extension for what could otherwise be an individualized, case-by-case determination for each parcel or allottee whose trust period is expiring in calendar year 1939 — a real second-order effect on affected individual Indian landowners' path toward fee-simple title, resolved administratively at scale rather than parcel-by-parcel, though the practice recites long-standing statutory grounding and is not itself novel to this order.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; the order expressly authorizes the Secretary of War to release designated sections to the Civil Aeronautics Authority for civil-aircraft crossing when not needed for defense purposes — a non-interference/release-back clause matching this stream's established protective-design pattern.

title: Executive Order 8005—Authorizing the Inspection of Income, Excess-Profits, and Capital Stock Tax Returns, Estate and Gift Tax Returns Filed After June 16, 1933, and Returns Under Title IX of the Social Security Act

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements an extensive but precisely-cited statutory chain (seven separate Revenue Acts plus the Social Security Act) authorizing tax-return inspection; inspection is expressly conditioned on compliance with Treasury Decision rules approved contemporaneously by the President, not open executive discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully cited statutory basis and a companion, contemporaneously-approved Treasury Decision governing the actual inspection procedure.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Consolidates authorization across multiple statutes and return types into a single coordinating order, but all serve the single integrated purpose of authorizing lawful inspection under a disclosed procedural framework, not combining unrelated matters.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Inspection is bounded by the referenced Treasury Decision's rules and regulations, not left to undefined discretion.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing authorization tied to the referenced statutes, ordinary maintenance.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8006—Inspection of Income and Excess-Profits Tax Returns by the Special Joint Congressional Committee to Make an Investigation of the Tennessee Valley Authority

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, single-purpose authorization enabling a specific Congressional investigating committee (per Public Resolution 83) to inspect tax returns for a bounded set of tax years, in furtherance of legislative oversight rather than executive power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, bounded purpose, and an explicit Federal Register publication requirement for transparency.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Inspection is conditioned on the same referenced Treasury Decision rules, not open discretion.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Bounded to the stated investigation and specified tax years.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order affirmatively facilitates, rather than preempts, Congressional oversight of the executive branch (TVA) — a positive accountability feature.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Recurring seasonal need is met through a bounded, openly-disclosed temporary category rather than an open-ended bypass; no perverse incentive is created since the positions are inherently short-term intermittent labor.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicit, fixed date range requiring a fresh order for any future season — a well-designed bounded exception, consistent with the ordinary seasonal-exception template.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight

title: Executive Order 8016—Amending the Foreign Service Regulations of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, procedural implementation of existing constitutional treaty-making practice (the 'alternat' protocol, full-powers exchange, ratification transmittal procedure); no discretionary authority is created beyond ordinary diplomatic-agent instructions.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Highly detailed, itemized procedural rules leave minimal room for inconsistent application.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Procedures are precisely specified (signature order, document formatting, cabling requirements), not left to vague interpretation.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing procedural regulation, ordinary maintenance.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The order affirmatively and explicitly preserves the Senate's constitutional advice-and-consent role in treaty ratification ('all treaties signed on the part of the United States are subject to ratification by and with the advice and consent of the Senate') — a positive, self-limiting accountability feature, not a removal of oversight. It also transparently identifies which prior regulation sections (IX-1 to IX-7) are being superseded.

title: Executive Order 8026—Postponement of Effective Date of Certain Provisions of Executive Order No. 6166 of June 10, 1933

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, transparent citation to the specific provision being postponed and to the reorganization order's own postponement mechanism (section 22).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Thirteenth confirmed instance in this stream's chronic reorganization-postponement chain (EO 6166's own consolidation/disbursement-transfer provisions, first postponed in EO 6224 and repeatedly re-postponed through EO 6540, 6727, 6927, 7077, 7261, 7390, 7526, 7639, 7774, 7917, 7980, now 8026), each time deferring rather than resolving the underlying reorganization question — a sustained absence of a genuine sunset on the postponement power itself, now spanning more than five years without final resolution.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; each postponement is a fully disclosed, individually issued order.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, bounded positions, retained CSC qualification-review check, explicit no-status proviso.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Avoids competitive process for these initial hires, though narrowly bounded to a one-time agency-startup context, matching the pattern already established by this division's companion order EO 7963.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Expressly limited to 'initial appointments' only — once made, the exception is exhausted, unlike an ongoing bypass authority.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The retained CSC qualification-review requirement and the Administrator's on-record recommendation together supply a meaningful accountability anchor, identical in structure to EO 7963.

title: Executive Order 8029—Documents Required of Aliens Entering the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements the 1918/1921 statutory framework governing entry documentation for immigrants and nonimmigrants; the extensive itemized exceptions (Parts I-II) are specific, bounded categories (returning residents, children born abroad, transit passengers) rather than a concentration of undefined discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Detailed, itemized categories and a companion procedural framework (Parts III-VI) delegating narrow administrative implementation to named territorial officials and the Secretaries of State and Labor.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Consolidates immigrant and nonimmigrant documentary rules, territorial administration, and a general savings/definitions framework into one order, but all sections serve the single integrated subject of alien entry documentation; the order also transparently identifies which prior orders it does and does not supersede.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The provision authorizing the Secretary of State (and, for the Virgin Islands, the Governor) to waive passport and visa requirements 'in his discretion... in cases of emergency' supplies no defined criteria for what qualifies as an emergency, leaving a materially discretionary waiver power without an articulated standard — an enforcement-vagueness hook distinct from the itemized, bounded exceptions elsewhere in the order.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing regulatory framework implementing a permanent statute, subject to supersession by future orders as it itself supersedes EO 7865.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; the order is itself the primary disclosed regulatory framework, not a carve-out from an existing check.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Broad category-wide reach (all aliens entering the U.S.) is fully itemized by disclosed exception categories rather than left to unpredictable downstream effects.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.
